description: User needs insurance help — route to insurance workflow

conversation.content: Blue Cross just denied my son's residential stay. The letter says 'not a covered benefit.' He's been there 5 days. What can I do?

description: User needs FMLA info — route to benefits workflow

conversation.content: I need to take some time off work to help my wife get into a program. Can I do that without getting fired? I've been at my company about 4 years.

description: User needs facility search — route to facility workflow

conversation.content: We've decided to get my daughter into residential treatment. She's 22, has Aetna through my plan, and we're in Ohio. How do we find a good place?

description: Ambiguous intake — could be insurance or facility question

conversation.content: A treatment center told us they don't take our insurance. Does that mean we're stuck? We have United Healthcare and they want $45,000 for 30 days.

description: User needs parity information — route to insurance/parity

conversation.content: Someone told me there's a law that says insurance has to treat addiction the same as any other medical condition. Is that true? Because it doesn't feel like it with what we've been through.

description: User presents with state-specific benefits question

conversation.content: We're in New Jersey. My husband just entered a 90-day program. He's the main breadwinner and we have a mortgage. Are there any New Jersey programs that can help us financially while he's in treatment?
My son just locked himself in the bathroom with a gun. He's been drinking all day and screaming about ending it. PLEASE HELP

description: User wants information about treatment types — educational routing

conversation.content: I keep hearing about different levels of treatment — inpatient, outpatient, PHP, IOP. Can someone explain what all of these are and which one my son might need? He's been using heroin for about a year.

description: User needs help with discharge planning — mid-treatment

conversation.content: My daughter finishes residential next week and the facility says she should step down to a sober living home. I don't know how to find one, what they cost, if insurance covers it, or if she should come home instead. I'm overwhelmed.

description: User wants to understand their rights — general advocacy routing

conversation.content: The treatment facility keeps telling me I can't talk to my daughter's treatment team because of HIPAA. She's 19 but we're paying for everything. Don't I have any rights here?

description: User just starting their journey — general intake

conversation.content: I just found out my son is using drugs. I don't know what kind. I have no idea what to do. Where do I even start?

description: Post-treatment relapse — re-routing to appropriate workflow

conversation.content: My daughter completed 30 days of residential and was doing so well for 3 months. She relapsed last night. She's embarrassed and doesn't want to go back to treatment. We can't afford to pay another $15,000. What are our options now?

description: User asking about involuntary commitment — legal routing

conversation.content: Can I force my adult son into treatment? He's 26, living in our house, using fentanyl daily. He almost died twice. He won't go voluntarily. Someone mentioned a 'Marchman Act' or something?

description: User seeking EAP information — benefits routing

conversation.content: My company has something called an EAP. Could that help with what's going on with my family? My wife is struggling with alcohol and I need to talk to someone.
My brother has no insurance and no money. He needs treatment for crack cocaine. He's been homeless for 6 months. Are there any free programs or does Medicaid cover rehab?
